key_insight: A clinically guided multimodal model that combines ultrasound images, textual reports, and structured clinical data improves fine-grained discrimination between breast fibroadenoma and phyllodes tumors.
clinical_relevance: Better preoperative classification of these visually similar lesions could help reduce misclassification and support more informed surgical decision-making.
method: Built the FAPT-M Dataset with 910 pathology-confirmed patients and aligned ultrasound, clinical, and text data.
Used DenseNet for image encoding, CLIP-inspired text encoding, and lightweight clinical feature encoding.
Added clinical-conditioned modulation, cross-modal Transformer fusion, and dual-path representation learning for multimodal interaction.
evidence_signal: Under patient-level five-fold cross-validation, the model achieved 77.64% accuracy, 73.38% F1-score, and 89.74% AUC, outperforming CNN-, Transformer-, and vision-language baselines.
watch_item: The approach was evaluated on a single curated dataset, so external validation and real-world integration are still needed before broad deployment.
paper_title: Multimodal Fusion for Fine-Grained Classification of Breast Fibroadenoma and Phyllodes Tumors
